title: Implement SharePoint document download via Graph API
description:
## Context

Download .docx files from SharePoint Online using Microsoft Graph API. Requires auth token from device code flow task.

## Implementation Details

- Parse SharePoint URLs to extract site ID, drive ID, and item ID
  - Handle formats: sharing links, direct URLs, site-relative paths
- Use Graph API endpoints:
  - GET /sites/{siteId}/drives/{driveId}/items/{itemId}/content (download)
  - GET /sites/{hostname}:/path (resolve site by URL)
  - GET /drives/{driveId}/items/{itemId} (metadata)
- Download document to temp directory
- Support SharePoint sharing links (resolve via /shares endpoint)
- Handle large files with streaming download
- Return local file path for conversion pipeline

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Downloads .docx from SharePoint given a URL
- [ ] Handles various SharePoint URL formats
- [ ] Handles sharing links
- [ ] Streams large files without excess memory usage
- [ ] Clear error on permission denied / not found

## Testing

- Unit tests with mocked Graph API responses
- Test URL parsing with various SharePoint URL formats

title: Write Claude Code skill manifest (skill.md)
description:
## Context

Package the tool as a Claude Code skill so it can be invoked as /sharepoint-to-md within Claude Code sessions.

## Implementation Details

- Create skill.md with:
  - Skill name, description, trigger patterns
  - Instructions for Claude Code on how to invoke the binary
  - Parameter documentation
  - Example invocations
- Create install.sh or setup instructions for:
  - Building the Go binary
  - Installing pandoc (brew install pandoc)
  - Azure AD app registration guide (for SharePoint URL mode)
- Add to ~/.claude/skills/ symlink or direct placement

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] skill.md follows Claude Code skill format
- [ ] /sharepoint-to-md invocable from Claude Code
- [ ] Setup instructions are complete and tested

## Notes

Reference existing skills in ~/.claude/skills/ for format conventions.

title: Scaffold Go CLI project with cobra and Makefile
description:
## Context

Set up the Go project structure for sharepoint-to-md with cobra CLI framework and standard Makefile.

## Implementation Details

- Initialize Go module (github.com/user/sharepoint-to-md or internal path)
- Set up cobra root command with flags: --file, --url, --output, --images-dir
- Create Makefile with: build, test, lint, clean targets
- Project layout: cmd/, internal/, main.go
- Add .gitignore for Go binaries

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] go build produces working binary
- [ ] --help shows all flags with descriptions
- [ ] make build/test/lint all work
- [ ] Project structure follows Go conventions

title: Implement Microsoft Graph API auth with device code flow
description:
## Context

Enable authenticated access to SharePoint Online documents via Microsoft Graph API. Device code flow is ideal for CLI tools — no web server or redirect URI needed.

## Implementation Details

- Use the Microsoft Authentication Library (MSAL) for Go or direct OAuth2 device code flow
- Register an Azure AD (Entra ID) app with permissions: Files.Read.All, Sites.Read.All
- Implement device code flow: POST to /devicecode endpoint, poll /token endpoint
- Display user-friendly instructions ("Visit https://microsoft.com/devicelogin and enter code XXXXXX")
- Cache tokens to ~/.config/sharepoint-to-md/token.json (with refresh token support)
- Token refresh on expiry without re-prompting
- Support configurable tenant ID (for org-specific apps)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Device code flow prompts user and obtains access token
- [ ] Token cached and reused across invocations
- [ ] Token refresh works transparently
- [ ] Clear error messages for auth failures
- [ ] Configurable client ID and tenant ID via flags or env vars

## Testing

- Integration test with mock OAuth2 server
- Unit tests for token caching/refresh logic

## Notes

Azure app registration is a one-time manual step (documented in README). The skill handles everything after that.

title: Wire up end-to-end CLI pipeline
description:
## Context

Connect all components into a cohesive CLI flow: parse args → acquire document → convert → output.

## Implementation Details

- Two code paths based on input:
  1. --file: skip auth, go straight to pandoc conversion
  2. --url: authenticate → download from SharePoint → convert
- Output handling:
  - Default: write to stdout
  - --output flag: write to specified file
  - --images-dir flag: extract images to directory
- Progress feedback for long operations (download, conversion)
- Clean temp file handling (defer cleanup)
- Exit codes: 0 success, 1 conversion error, 2 auth error, 3 network error

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] sharepoint-to-md --file doc.docx produces markdown on stdout
- [ ] sharepoint-to-md --file doc.docx -o out.md writes to file
- [ ] sharepoint-to-md --url "https://..." authenticates and converts
- [ ] Temp files cleaned up on success and failure
- [ ] Meaningful exit codes and error messages

## Testing

- End-to-end test with local .docx file
- Integration test mocking Graph API for URL path

title: Add markdown post-processing and cleanup
description:
## Context

Pandoc output is good but sometimes needs cleanup for production-quality markdown. This task adds optional post-processing.

## Implementation Details

- Strip excessive blank lines (pandoc sometimes adds extra)
- Normalize heading levels if document starts at H3 (shift to H1)
- Clean up table formatting for readability
- Convert absolute image paths to relative
- Optionally strip Word-specific artifacts (e.g., []{.underline} spans)
- Make post-processing opt-in via --clean flag (default: on)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Excessive blank lines removed
- [ ] Heading levels normalized
- [ ] Image paths converted to relative
- [ ] Word artifacts stripped
- [ ] Can be disabled with --no-clean

## Testing

- Unit tests with known pandoc output samples
- Before/after comparison tests

title: Implement pandoc-based .docx to .md conversion
description:
## Context

Core conversion engine using pandoc to transform .docx files to markdown.

## Implementation Details

- Shell out to pandoc with appropriate flags: pandoc -f docx -t gfm input.docx
- Use GFM (GitHub Flavored Markdown) as target format
- Handle image extraction: --extract-media flag
- Capture stdout/stderr, handle pandoc errors gracefully
- Check pandoc is installed, provide helpful error if missing
- Support passing through additional pandoc flags via --pandoc-args

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Converts .docx to clean GFM markdown
- [ ] Images extracted to specified directory when requested
- [ ] Tables, lists, headings preserved correctly
- [ ] Clear error when pandoc not installed
- [ ] Handles large documents without hanging

## Testing

- Unit tests with sample .docx files
- Test error cases (missing pandoc, corrupt file, etc.)

title: Plan: sharepoint-to-md skill
description:
## Goal

CLI skill that converts SharePoint/Word documents to clean Markdown without losing essential text or formatting.

## Architecture

Two-layer approach:
1. **Document acquisition**: Microsoft Graph API (authenticated SharePoint download) OR local file input
2. **Document conversion**: Pandoc (.docx → .md) as primary engine, with optional image extraction

## Key Design Decisions

- **Pandoc over Google Docs/LibreOffice**: Direct .docx→.md is faster, simpler, and higher quality than multi-hop conversions
- **Microsoft Graph API over Playwright**: API is more reliable, scriptable, and maintainable than browser automation
- **Device code flow for auth**: No web server required, works in CLI context
- **Go CLI**: Matches codebase conventions

## Usage Patterns

```
sharepoint-to-md --file input.docx                          # Local file
sharepoint-to-md --url "https://company.sharepoint.com/..." # SharePoint URL
sharepoint-to-md --url "..." --images-dir ./img             # Extract images
```
